summary: Frontend-only retest executed on public URL for graph drag reliability and sorting comparison/playback restoration. Graph core interactions (add node, edge mode, BFS run, graph playback visibility) work. Drag behavior remains inconsistent in automation checks. Sorting comparison toggle is present and functional; playback button is present but stayed disabled while long-running sort execution remained active during test windows.

ui_bugs.issue: Drag reliability is still inconsistent in retest runs. Label-origin drag succeeded intermittently (2/3), and circle-origin drag failed in repeated attempts (0/3) under automation despite node movement working in some cases.

integration_issues.issue: Playback control remains visible, but stayed disabled while sorting execution continued for extended duration in tests; completion not reached within automation windows, so post-completion playback enablement could not be conclusively verified.

action_items: Re-validate GraphCanvas drag start handling across both label and circle hit areas with deterministic pointer-event handling (consider moving drag-start to node group with drag threshold logic).
Add a lightweight UI regression test that asserts node position change after drag initiated from both text and circle hit targets.
For sorting verification, add/confirm a short deterministic dataset or test mode so run completion and playback-enable transition can be reliably validated in automation.
critical_code_review_comments: GraphCanvas starts drag only from circle onMouseDown, while movement lifecycle relies on SVG mouse events; this can still be brittle for hit-target/event-sequence consistency across interactions.

Sorting playback button state is tied to isRunning || !steps.length, so long-running sessions keep playback disabled for extended periods; this is expected by code, but makes restoration verification timing-sensitive.
context_for_next_testing_agent: Executed multiple Playwright retests on https://visualgo-pro.preview.emergentagent.com. Graph: add node, edge mode (weight prompt), BFS start, and graph playback control are working. Drag checks were inconsistent: one run failed label drag, another run passed label drag but failed circle drag, and reliability matrix produced label [true,false,true], circle [false,false,false]. Sorting: comparison toggle/config consistently visible. Playback button is present but remained disabled during active long-running sort sessions in all attempts; could not confirm post-completion enablement within automation window.
rca of the issue: Graph drag inconsistency appears tied to pointer event sequence/hit-target handling during drag initiation and move lifecycle in SVG interactions. Reproduction: create nodes on /graph and drag node A repeatedly from label and circle; observe non-deterministic movement start. Sorting playback behavior observed as state-gated by isRunning; when sessions are long, playback remains disabled until run completes, which can be misread as regression without completion synchronization.
